concepts.title: Carbohydrates are the most abundant biomolecules
concepts.description: Carbohydrates are the largest group of biomolecules and are found in all living organisms. They are the main source of energy for the body and are involved in various physiological processes.

concepts.title: Monomers of proteins are amino acids
concepts.description: Amino acids are the building blocks of proteins and are linked together by peptide bonds to form a polypeptide chain.

concepts.title: DNA structure is double-stranded and twisted
concepts.description: The DNA structure is a double helix, with two complementary strands twisted together. This twisted structure is stabilized by hydrogen bonds between the nitrogenous bases.

concepts.title: Mnemonic for the structure of DNA
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'Double Helix, Nitrogen Bases' to remember the structure of DNA, with the two strands twisted together and the nitrogenous bases paired.

concepts.title: Difference between monosaccharides and disaccharides
concepts.description: Students often confuse monosaccharides and disaccharides, but it's essential to remember that monosaccharides are simple sugars, whereas disaccharides are formed by the condensation of two monosaccharides.

concepts.title: Molecular formula for DNA
concepts.description: The molecular formula for DNA is (C5H5N2O2)x, where x is the number of nucleotide units in the DNA molecule.

concepts.title: Starch and glycogen are polysaccharides
concepts.description: Starch and glycogen are two types of polysaccharides that serve as energy storage molecules in plants and animals, respectively.

concepts.title: Amino acids have both acidic and basic groups
concepts.description: Amino acids have both an amino group (-NH2) and a carboxyl group (-COOH), which makes them amphoteric in nature.
